issue_title: Native SQL Schema names broken on MySQL
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

When trying to delete a Role while running on MySQL, an error is thrown complaining about invalid SQL syntax, because the schema name is being wrapped in double quotes.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

Should be able to delete realm without throwing a SQL error

### Actual behavior

An error is thrown and the Role is not deleted.

### How to Reproduce?

Run Keycloak on MySQL/MariaDB

### Anything else?

The bug was introduced in this commit: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/commit/dc9970f578144557d75e6e1e21f57dcdf6c70f38
commit_message:
Use the appropriate database dialect to add quotes to the schema name (#26964)

Closes #25961

Signed-off-by: Alexander Schwartz <aschwart@redhat.com>

issue_title: Failing to import client's authorisation settings through UI
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Unable to import client authorisation settings.

### Version

20.0.1

### Expected behavior

importing another client's authorisation settings into client should create the respective resource, policies, scopes and permissions

### Actual behavior

importing another client's authorisation settings into client does not create the respective resource, policies, scopes and permissions 

### How to Reproduce?

1. create client "test" with client authn and authz turned on
2. head over to "test" client Authorization tab
3. delete the default policy and resource
4. create new resource "A-resource"
5. create client policy "A-policy" with account client
6. create resource permission (A-permission) with A-resource tagged to A-policy
7. export the client's authz settings
8. create client "test2" with client authn and authz turned on
9. import the exported "test" client authz settings to test2
10. head over to test2 Authorization tab.
11. It will be still the same state when it was created. A-resource, A-policy and A-permission are not exported

### Anything else?

Hihi just to update. I tried with keycloak 23.0.3. The import still doesnt work. HOWEVER it only works if its imported into the same client that it exported out from. Is this suppose to be the behavior?

_No response_
commit_message:
Do not export ids when exporting authorization settings

Closes #25975

issue_title: No error message displayed when trying to add read-only attribute to some user in `Attributes` tab
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

- Enable user profile
- Enable switch `Unmanaged attributes` to `Enabled` for some realm
- Create some user. He has enabled `Attributes` tab in the admin console, which is correct
- Try to add attribute `LDAP_ID` and click `Save`. The server returns 400, which is correct behaviour. However UI does not display any error message. Would it be better if UI display some better error message for this case?

Just a note that I don't need LDAP provider created in my realm and I don't need attribute `LDAP_ID` to be defined on user profile. The attribute `LDAP_ID` is one of the attributes specified as read-only attribute in the user profile provider configuration https://www.keycloak.org/docs/latest/server_admin/index.html#read_only_user_attributes .

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

UI display some error message when the 400 is returned from the server when clicking `Save` on the `Attributes` page of some user

### Actual behavior

Nothing is displayed to the administrator, so he doesn't know if attribute was successfully added to the user etc (unless he monitors server log where is WARN message displayed).

### How to Reproduce?

See above

### Anything else?

_No response_

Read Only Access of a realm clients' Authz is broken for Admin Console
Fixes #24586

issue_title: Force reauthentication is ignored during identity brokering when mapping between OIDC and SAML protocols
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

### Summary
Keycloak doesn't correctly map OIDC `prompt=login` to a SAML `ForceAuthN` during identity brokering.

### Detail
The OIDC specification states that when a server receives a `prompt=login` authentication request parameter the server:

> The Authorization Server SHOULD prompt the End-User for reauthentication.

Therefore, when a client initiates an OIDC authentication request with the protocol `prompt=login` parameter and the request results in a SAML identity broker redirection (for instance, using the Identity Provider Redirector), Keycloak should map the OIDC `prompt=login` to a SAML `ForceAuthN` XML attribute. This ensures that the external SAML provider also prompts the user for reauthentication regardless of session state. 

Currently, the `SAMLIdentityProvider` logic will ignore any dynamic state in the request context, such as the OIDC parameter, and instead reads from the Identity Provider configuration value to build the SAML request using `ForceAuthN`. Therefore, `ForceAuthN` will only be sent if the IdP has been permanently configured to do so, which makes it hard for clients to conditionally opt-in to the behaviour.

This is a common scenario for step-up authentication flows when an OIDC client needs to ensure that the user is reauthenticated regardless of their session state. 

### Fix
Refactor the logic in the `SAMLIdentityProvider#performLogin()` method to conditionally override the IdP `forceAuthN` value based on the authentication session state and whether `prompt=login` was passed.

### More detail
Code flow for the IdentityProviderAuthenticator, If an IdP is found, it does a redirect here:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5c86abbc32a7425b7f465e6d51806fa8650a231a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/authentication/authenticators/browser/IdentityProviderAuthenticator.java#L86

This creates a URL which is created to send the request to the RealmsResource-> IdentityBrokerService->`performLogin` method:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5c86abbc32a7425b7f465e6d51806fa8650a231a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/Urls.java#L56

That service is responsible for loading the IdentityProvider (e.g. SAMLIdentityProvider) and calling the `performLogin` method to create the redirect:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5c86abbc32a7425b7f465e6d51806fa8650a231a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/IdentityBrokerService.java#L363

The SAMLIdentityProvider creates the SAML2 request, specifically the ability to use `ForceAuthn` based on the Config, which is associated with the identity provider, rather than a transient value which can be passed.
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/5c86abbc32a7425b7f465e6d51806fa8650a231a/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/broker/saml/SAMLIdentityProvider.java#L164


### Note
As this is a simple fix, I am raising this issue purely as a pointer to reference in the commit and PR and I will be submitting a Pr to fix shortly.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

When a client sends requests an OIDC authentication request with the protocol `prompt=login` parameter and the request results in a SAML identity broker redirection, Keycloak should map the OIDC `prompt=login` to a SAML `ForceAuthN` attribute.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak ignores the `prompt=login` parameter and generates a SAML request without `ForceAuthN`.

### How to Reproduce?

- Configure a SAML IdP with `ForceAuthN` set to `false` and associate with a user
- Configure an authentication flow with the "Identity Provider Redirector"
- Configure an OIDC client and bind to authentication flow
- Send OIDC auth request with `prompt=login`
- Get redirected to external SAML IdP and note lack of `ForceAuthN` in the SAML request XML


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Ensure protocol forced reauthentication is correctly mapped during SAML identity brokering

Closes #25980

Signed-off-by: Patrick Hamann <patrick@fastly.com>

issue_title: PostgreSQL deadlock causes 400 client error instead of 500 server error
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

If two clients happen to update realm attributes exactly at the same time, it may cause a [deadlock](https://www.cybertec-postgresql.com/en/postgresql-understanding-deadlocks/) at SQL server (PostgreSQL in our case).  In this scenario, Keycloak may return unexpected error response and also "leak" the SQL query details in error response body.

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

In this error scenario:

1. Keycloak should preferably return `500 Internal Server Error` to indicate that client may retry the operation. 
2. The error response from Keycloak should not contain SQL query details.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak returns `400 Bad Request` with following response body:

```json
{"errorMessage":"jakarta.persistence.OptimisticLockException: org.hibernate.exception.LockAcquisitionException: could not execute statement [ERROR: deadlock detected\n  Detail: Process 182 waits for ShareLock on transaction 2310; blocked by process 176.\nProcess 176 waits for ShareLock on transaction 2309; blocked by process 182.\n  Hint: See server log for query details.\n  Where: while deleting tuple (3,71) in relation \"realm_attribute\"] [delete from REALM_ATTRIBUTE where NAME=? and REALM_ID=?]"}
```

Luckily, Hibernate uses prepared statements here, so no sensitive data is leaked.


### How to Reproduce?

Note that following steps are not a realistic use case, but it provides easy way to reproduce the fault just in a few seconds.

I've used following short Python scripts below to update the realm attributes concurrently in two separate processes. This is eventually likely to cause a deadlock at the database server.

<details>

This first script fetches token and full realm data, and then it updates whole realm by PUTting the fetched realm data back in a busy loop:

```python
import requests

TOKEN = requests.post(
    'http://keycloak/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token',
    data={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin', 'grant_type': 'password', 'client_id': 'admin-cli'}
).json()['access_token']

REALM_DATA = requests.get(
    'http://keycloak/admin/realms/master',
    headers={'Authorization': 'bearer ' + TOKEN}).text

num_requests = 1
while True:
    res = requests.put(
        'http://keycloak/admin/realms/master',
        headers={'Authorization': 'bearer ' + TOKEN,
                 'Content-Type': 'application/json'},
        data=REALM_DATA)
    if res.status_code == 204:
        print(f'Attempt {num_requests} successful')
    else:
        print(f'Error at attempt {num_requests}: {res.status_code} {res.reason}: {res.text}')
    num_requests += 1
```

The above script alone will not cause errors, it will always succeed (well, until token expires). One client triggers only one SQL transaction which cannot cause deadloc. But if running another client concurrently that updates couple of other independent attributes in the same table, then database deadlock happens in a few seconds:

```python
import requests

TOKEN = requests.post(
    'http://keycloak/realms/master/protocol/openid-connect/token',
    data={'username': 'admin', 'password': 'admin', 'grant_type': 'password', 'client_id': 'admin-cli'}
).json()['access_token']

num_requests = 1
while True:
    res = requests.put(
        'http://keycloak/admin/realms/master',
        headers={'Authorization': 'bearer ' + TOKEN},
        json={'bruteForceProtected': True, 'defaultSignatureAlgorithm': 'ES384'}
    )
    if res.status_code == 204:
        print(f'Attempt {num_requests} successful')
    else:
        print(f'Error at attempt {num_requests}: {res.status_code} {res.reason}: {res.text}')
    num_requests += 1
```

Output Example

```
Error at attempt 188: 400 Bad Request: {"errorMessage":"jakarta.persistence.OptimisticLockException: org.hibernate.exception.LockAcquisitionException: could not execute statement [ERROR: deadlock detected\n  Detail: Process 239 waits for ShareLock on transaction 2567; blocked by process 245.\nProcess 245 waits for ShareLock on transaction 2566; blocked by process 239.\n  Hint: See server log for query details.\n  Where: while deleting tuple (94,36) in relation \"realm_attribute\"] [delete from REALM_ATTRIBUTE where NAME=? and REALM_ID=?]"}
```

</details>


### Anything else?

While running the reproduction steps above, it can be seen that the second python script that updates individual attributes will get error code `500 Internal Server Error` with response body `{"error":"unknown_error"}` which seems better, compared to the first python script that gets `400 Bad Request`. 

When enabling the SQL statement debug on PostgreSQL side, it can be observed that the first script (update of full realm) will generate SQL transaction with `DELETE` and `INSERT` for `REALM_ATTRIBUTE` i.e. it seems to delete and re-import the whole realm. The second script that updates just two individual attributes will generate smaller SQL transaction with only `UPDATE` statements in it.

I guess DB deadlock might happen with other types of scenarios too, but in our case we bumped into this via realm attribute updates.

I attach two example backtraces from Keycloak when the SQL deadlock happens

a) PostgreSQL exception: org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: ERROR: deadlock detected

issue_title: Identity provider sync mode: incorrect selection in case of null
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/ui

### Describe the bug

Identity provider sync mode shows 'Import' selection when the value is not stored on the backend.
![изображение](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/1285898/2509d0f0-9e4e-4798-ab5e-e8ca021a6614)

However backend uses the `Legacy` value in this case (see `org.keycloak.models.IdentityProviderModel.getSyncMode()`)

### Version

22.0.5, 23.0.3

### Expected behavior

UI should show the select value for 'Sync mode' synchronous with backend (Legacy)

### Actual behavior

UI shows 'Import' as the selected value for 'Sync mode' which is inconsistent with the actual backend value.

### How to Reproduce?

2 ways to reproduce:

1) Create an identity provider via UI, save it ONCE. 
NOTE: During saving there is no possibility to select the sync mode (why?), so it's not saved to the database.
Then check the UI, it shows 'Import' as the selected value, but the backend acts in 'Legacy' mode

2) Update from Keycloak 8.0.1 to the actual version. No 'syncMode' properties exist for current identity providers.
Reason: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/6705 didn't introduce any Liquibase migrations.

### Anything else?

If we create an identity provider from UI and save it twice, then the UI value is synchronous with backend (because UI already renders the 'Sync mode' selectbox and second save stores it to the db.
commit_message:
Present effective sync mode value

When sync mode value is missing in the config of newly created identity
provider, the provider does not store any. When no value is
found, the identity provider behaves as if `LEGACY` was used (#6705).

This PR ensures the correct sync mode is returned from the REST endpoint,
regardless of whether it has been stored in the database or not.

Fixes: #26019

Signed-off-by: Hynek Mlnarik <hmlnarik@redhat.com>

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

In the account console v3, the refresh button on the `Account security` -> `Device activity` page has an unnecessary tooltip that exactly matches the label of the button.

### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

The tooltip should either display a more verbose explanation of the button or not be displayed at all.

### Actual behavior

![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/5558742/e2ad0b8c-cd90-4f37-aa18-1564e0542879)


### How to Reproduce?

Open the account console v3 (enabled by default in nightly), navigate to `Account security` -> `Device activity`, and mouse over or tab to the "Refresh the page" button.

Account console v2 uses the translation key `refresh` for the label and `refreshPage` for the tooltip:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/55d0a32b09a4e2d32f689c7b8ed0b864ca812854/themes/src/main/resources/theme/keycloak.v2/account/src/app/content/device-activity-page/DeviceActivityPage.tsx#L228-L238

Account console v3 uses `refreshPage` for both:
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/55d0a32b09a4e2d32f689c7b8ed0b864ca812854/js/apps/account-ui/src/account-security/DeviceActivity.tsx#L118-L128

Using the shorter "Refresh" for the label probably makes sense, but the translation key would have to be copied from v2 to v3 (including localizations).

issue_title: Name ID Format field is confusing for User Attribute Mapper For NameID
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

There is a SAML mapper named: `User Attribute Mapper For NameID`. The purpose of this mapper is to set the value of the `NameID` element in assertions produced by Keycloak.

For the mapper, it is possible to configure `Name ID Format`. The tooltip for this says: [Name ID Format using Mapper
](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/70e15bdaa49a8babc5dd76882ca5c66462e548fe/js/apps/admin-ui/maven-resources/theme/keycloak.v2/admin/messages/messages_en.properties#L496)

This isn't very clear because users may think this mapper also changes `NameID.Format` of the resulting assertion while this field is used to filter when the mapper should be applied: [only if the `AuthnRequest`'s `NameIDPolicy` is equal to the value of this field](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/70e15bdaa49a8babc5dd76882ca5c66462e548fe/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/saml/SamlProtocol.java#L614C5-L614C5).

### Example of how the mapper works:
There are two nameId mappers:
1. `mapper.nameid.format = email, user.attribute = custom_email_attribute`
2. `mapper.nameid.format = unspecified, user.attribute = another_custom_attribute`

Then we have a user:
`Username: testUsername, custom_email_attribute = test@username.com, another_custom_attribute = foo`

#### Scenario 1
The client sends an authentication request with following NameId policy:
`<samlp:NameIDPolicy Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress" />`

in this case:
Keycloak sees that the client expects a response with `emailAddress` NameID format. When responding it will query all `NameIdMappers` and find out there is mapper number 1. with the mapper.nameid.format equal to `emailAddress`. Therefore it applies the mapper by obtaining the custom_email_address:
`<saml:NameID  Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress">test@username.com</saml:NameID>`


#### Scenario 2 
The client sends an authentication request with the following NameId policy:
`<samlp:NameIDPolicy Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified" />`

In this case Keycloak uses mapper 2 with the result:
`<saml:NameID  Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified">foo</saml:NameID>`


#### Scenario 3
If another NameIdPolicy is sent, for example: `urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:nameid-format:persistent` no mapper is applied because there is no mapper for such format present.

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

The tooltip should better explain this

### Actual behavior

The tooltip says only: Name ID Format using Mapper

### How to Reproduce?

1. Open the admin console and go to Client Scope
2. Create a new Client Scope with Protocol `SAML`
3. Open the Client Scope -> Mappers
4. Configure a new mapper -> User Attribute Mapper For NameID
5. You should see the following page: 
![image](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/assets/13901995/7d603332-21a0-48b1-86c7-8fd0a8e13afc)


### Anything else?

Also, the field `Name ID Format` lists NameID formats even when they are not supported by Keycloak and `AuthnRequest`s with other formats are refused. Maybe we should not list those there?
https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/70e15bdaa49a8babc5dd76882ca5c66462e548fe/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/saml/SamlService.java#L470-L477
commit_message:
Fix confusing SAML NameId mapper format tooltip

Closes #26051
Signed-off-by: Michal Hajas <mhajas@redhat.com>
Co-authored-by: Hynek Mlnařík <hmlnarik@users.noreply.github.com>

issue_title: Configure OTP Form regenerates Secret on reload
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

OTP secret is regenerated on every reload of configure otp page.

When configuring an otp device the user scans the QR code and enters the code from the app and an optional device label.
If the scan of the QR code is successful but the user enters a wrong code and clicks save, the page reloads, displays an error and shows a new QR code.
So the user has to re-scan the QR code and try again. Just using the code from the app without a re-scan results in an invalid otp error.
This happens on every page reload (F5) and is very unintuitive. That is why I consider it to be a bug instead of a feature request or an enhancement.

### Version

23.0.4

### Expected behavior

The secret is only generated once and does not change between page loads.



### Actual behavior

OTP secret is regenerated on every reload of configure otp page.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Open configure OTP page
2. Scan the QR code
3. enter a wrong code and click save
4. enter the code from the app and click save again

### Anything else?

Maybe we can generate the totpSecret, store it e.g. as an authNote and pass it to the constructor of TotpBean (has to be extended) in https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/forms/login/freemarker/FreeMarkerLoginFormsProvider.java#L236?
commit_message:
Avoid regenerating the totpSecret on every reload of the OTP configuration page

Using an auth note to store the totpSecret and passing its value in the TotpBean constructor to keep the totpSecret on page reload

Closes #26052

issue_title: Device verification flow does not require consent under certain circumstances
issue_body:
### Description

Keycloak does not require consent in the device verification flow if there are no scopes. This could allow an attacker to gain a user's session without the user's knowledge or consent under some circumstances. The OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant spec requires user interaction (i.e., the user has clicked a button to give permission). 

### Version

\>= 23.0.4

### Steps to reproduce

Reproducer:

1. Create a realm, a user, a client.
2. Enable "OAuth 2.0 Device Authorization Grant Enabled"
3. Remove the "Assigned Default Client Scopes"
4. Log in as a user
5. Make a device flow request (With the scope offline_access, but that work also without) [3]
6. Call the `verification_uri_complete` URL (Keycloak say "Device Login Successful")
7. Make a `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code` flow request, to check that we have the access_token and refresh_token [4]

<!-- Comment to interrupt the list -->

1. Code Reference: 
```
<https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/48835576daa158443f69917ac309e1a7c951bc87/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/managers/AuthenticationManager.java#L1061-L1065>
```
2. Spec Reference: <https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8628#section-3.3>
```
    > Authorization servers supporting this specification **MUST** implement a
    > user-interaction sequence that starts with the user navigating to
    > "verification_uri" and continues with them supplying the "user_code"
    > at some stage during the interaction. Other than that, the exact
    > sequence and implementation of the user interaction is up to the
    > authorization server; for example, the authorization server may
    > enable new users to sign up for an account during the authorization
    > flow or add additional security verification steps.
```
3. Device Flow Request:
```
    curl --request POST \
    --url http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/realm/protocol/openid-connect/auth/device \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
    --data client_id=test \
    --data scope=offline_access
```
4. `urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code` flow request:
```
    curl --request POST \
    --url http://localhost:8080/auth/realms/realm/protocol/openid-connect/token \
    --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
    --data client_id=test \
    --data device_code= Y-quHfHLvfUa9cZj-LmLTRmPa6SLgq2FSvhi2_Pb7To \
    --data grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:device_code
    
```

### References: 
- https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHSSO-2682

issue_title: Improper Input Validation for Recovery Codes Setup
issue_body:
### Description

When an authenticated user sets up Recovery Codes, he has to validate a form listing the 12 Recovery Codes generated. The Recovery Codes displayed are sent back to the server when the user clicks the "Complete Setup" button though the generatedRecoveryAuthnCodes parameter. No checks on the values or number of the Recovery Codes sent by the user are performed by the application logic.

A user can therefore decide to send many more recovery codes (made of an alphabet of his choosing). If the user does not specify a parameter for the form's generatedRecoveryAuthnCodes parameter, a single Recovery Code equivalent to an empty string will be created.

### Version
\>= 23.0.4

## Recommendations

Consider revising the application logic to check the validity of Recovery Codes sent by the user against the server generated ones.

### References
- https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHSSO-2699
- [https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Input_Validation_Cheat_Sheet.html](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Input_Validation_Cheat_Sheet.html)

issue_title: Recovery Code Validation Race Possible
issue_body:
### Description

Recovery Codes are meant to be valid only once. The application logic deletes any Recovery Code used successfully. The foremost important method for this is org.keycloak.credential.RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialProvider#isValid The high level actions performed by this method are:

- Fetch and construct the RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel object from the database (wrapper class for the Recovery Codes defined for the user).
- If all Recovery Codes have not been used; Compare the user-supplied Recovery Code against the next one to be used (the user-supplied one is hashed with SHA256 and base-64 encoded before comparison) .
- If both Recovery Codes match; Remove the used Recovery Code from RecoveryAuthnCodesCredentialModel, persist the changes in the database, and return true.

Locking is not used to mark that method as a critical section to be accessed only by a thread at the time. Therefore, two requests can be processed in parallel by the isValid() method.

If both requests are processed in parallel, then the Recovery Codes for the user cannot be updated and persisted between the processing of requests. Said another way; Both requests will have the same Recovery Code to compare against if the remaining Recovery Codes are not yet persisted by the first request when the second request processing picks the Recovery Code it must compare with the user input.

Two different requests, originating from different HTTP clients but using the same credentials including the very same valid Recovery Code, emitted at the same time can both pass the Recovery Code validation phase of the authentication process.

Although mostly academic (given the timings necessary, limited impact, and prerequisites), this issue violates the expectation of a Recovery Code being valid only once.

### Version
\>= 23.0.4

### Recommendations

Consider defining the isValid() method as a critical section to prevent parallel execution of it for the same user.

### References
- https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHSSO-2696
- [https://portswigger.net/research/turbo-intruder-embracing-the-billion-request-attack](https://portswigger.net/research/turbo-intruder-embracing-the-billion-request-attack)
- [https://wiki.owasp.org/index.php/Testing_for_Race_Conditions_(OWASP-AT-010)](https://wiki.owasp.org/index.php/Testing_for_Race_Conditions_(OWASP-AT-010))

Unsanitized user input values that can contain special characters can be saved as the Realm ID and name. This can be done through the Keycloak admin console UI or though the Keycloak Admin REST API.

issue_title: Improper Input Validation and Sanitization Leads to persistent partial Denial of Service
issue_body:
### Description

The Keycloak REST API allows an authenticated realm admin to register new and non existent required actions for all users in a realm. This is done by adding the required action values in the body of an HTTP POST request to the /auth/admin/realms/master/authentication/register-required-action endpoint. However, no validation or sanitization is performed on the input values.

The improper validations allow adding any string value in the providerId field which is then stored in the database as the required action alias as well. After adding a non existent action as required, users can no longer log into the realm.

The improper sanitization could also result in a malformed URL affecting the functionality of other actions like the edition or deletion of the newly created required action using the PUT or DELETE HTTP methods through the /auth/admin/realms/[realm]/authentication/required-actions/[alias] endpoint.

### Version
\>= 23.0.4

### Impact

Unexistent required actions providerId (and alias) values leads to a persistent partial Denial of Service (DoS) by not allowing any user to login into the realm using the Keycloak UI. Not allowing a realm admin to delete, edit or perform any other action that requires the required action unsanitized alias in the URL path as a parameter persist this DoS. This issue gets more severe when the non existent required action has been added to the master realm and thus affects the access to it.

Manually deleting the required action from the REQUIRED_ACTION_PROVIDER table in the database and restarting the Keycloak server is the only way to restore the user access to the affected realm.

This issue has been classified as a weakness given that realm admin privileges are required to create the required action.

### Recommendations

* Validation should be performed before registering a new required action to ensure it exists and is properly implemented.
* A unified sanitization and validation strategy should be implemented on all user input values to ensure only valid values are accepted and saved.
* User input controlled values should not be used to unequivocally identify an object. Also, for this particular case, many required actions can share the same alias value at database (DB) level given that it hasn’t been defined as an UNIQUE DB field. Thus editing or deleting an action with a shared or duplicate alias could affect a different action than the initially intended. Using a Primary Key field (ID) or an UNIQUE Alternate Key is recommended as a best practice when deleting or editing objects from a database.

### References
- https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHSSO-2680
* https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org/cheatsheets/Input_Validation_Cheat_Sheet.html
* https://gitlab.cee.redhat.com/keycloak/keycloak-prod/-/blob/18.0.x/services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/services/resources/admin/AuthenticationManagementResource.java#L947

issue_title: Revoked Token may be valid for a short time after expiring
issue_body:
### Description

A timing issue in our token revocation and expiration within Keycloak OIDC. The mismatch occurs because the cache calculates token lifespans in milliseconds, whereas the expiry checks are in seconds. This mismatch leads to a one-second window where an expired token, already removed from the cache, is still erroneously considered valid. For the complexity to exploit this is considered a weakness. 

This is valid for one second and the malicious attacker must have access to the token in this meantime meaning it should already be compromised or fastly accessed. Also, after this timeframe the token is invalid forever and no further actions may be done. Confidentiality and Integrity are set as Low as this normally would affect a single user.

### Version
\>= 23.0.4

### References: 
- https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHSSO-2853
commit_message:
Revoked token cache expiration fix

Added 1 second to the duration of the cache for revoked tokens to prevent them from still being valid for 1 second after the expiration date of the access token.

Closes #26113

issue_title: Invalidate authentication session on repeated Recovery Code failures
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

authentication

### Describe the bug

At present, when a user is locked out by brute force protection the authentication session isn't invalidated. As a result when a temporary lockout expires an attacker can continue trying to guess the Recovery Code without reauthenticating. We should terminate the authentication session when the user is locked out by brute force.

### Version

999.0.0-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

When the user is locked out by brute force protection the auth session should end and the browser should be redirected to the login page.

### Actual behavior

When the user is locked out by brute force protection the auth session remains valid and the user can continue guessing recovery codes.

### How to Reproduce?

Configure recovery codes and enable brute force protection. Guess the recovery code enough times to get a temporary lockout of the user. Wait for the lockout to timeout and continue guessing recovery codes without reauthenticating.

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Invalidate authentication session on repeated Recovery Code failures
Closes #26180

issue_title: keycloak-admin-client missing recent changes to group query parameters
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

admin/client/node

### Describe the bug

The recently added query parameters `q` and `populateHierarchy` can't be passed to `adminClient.groups.find(payload)` without casting `payload` to `any`. 

### Version

23.0.3

### Expected behavior

The payload type `(Query & PaginatedQuery & SummarizedQuery & { realm?: string | undefined; })` must include `q` and `populateHierarchy`.

### Actual behavior

The payload type `(Query & PaginatedQuery & SummarizedQuery & { realm?: string | undefined; })` does not include `q` and `populateHierarchy` which leads to issues when using it in typescript.

### How to Reproduce?

create a new typescript project and add the dependency `@keycloak/keycloak-admin-client@23.0.4` 

```typescript
const credentials = {....}
const client = new KeycloakAdminClient({ realmName, baseUrl });
await client.auth(credentials);
const groups = await client.groups.find({ populateHierarchy: false, q: 'a:test' }); // this will result in a compile error
```

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

user-profile

### Describe the bug

When sso.redhat.com is migrating from their current version to upstream it happens that the user profile component does not have any configuration set and fails to migrate to upstream (999.0.0-SNAPSHOT) due to:

```
[org.keycloak.quarkus.runtime.cli.ExecutionExceptionHandler] (main) Error details:: java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "org.keycloak.representations.userprofile.config.UPConfig.clone()" because the return value of "org.keycloak.userprofile.DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.getConfigFromComponentModel(org.keycloak.component.ComponentModel)" is null        at org.keycloak.userprofile.DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.getConfiguration(DeclarativeUserProfileProvider.java:204)
        at org.keycloak.migration.migrators.MigrateTo24_0_0.updateUserProfileSettings(MigrateTo24_0_0.java:79)

        at org.keycloak.migration.migrators.MigrateTo24_0_0.lambda$migrate$0(MigrateTo24_0_0.java:40)
```

This might happen as in previous versions (not sure why, but a long time ago I would say) we were creating user profile component with empty configuration and making sure it would resolve the config from the default configuration in this case.

But now, the migration step for 24 is failing because it assumes the component always have a configuration set.

### Version

999-SNAPSHOT

### Expected behavior

Migration to 24 should happen without errors.

### Actual behavior

Errors when migrating to upstream.

### How to Reproduce?

Make sure a user profile component exists without a configuration and try to migrate.

issue_title: Reset credentials action fails when triggered from first broker login flow
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

identity-brokering

### Describe the bug

When a user logins for the first time using an external identity provider it is presented a flow to link this federated identity to a Keycloak user.

If the user does not remember its credentials and triggers the 'forgot password' flow. The user receives an email, but is presented with an error when clicking the link in the email.

### Version

main branch

### Expected behavior

The user is presented with the form to set his new password. After updating the password, the user should be linked to the federated identity and continue with the login process.

### Actual behavior

The form for setting a new password is not shown. An exception is thrown:

```
2024-01-18 14:48:45,489 WARN  [org.keycloak.services] (executor-thread-532) KC-SERVICES0013: Failed authentication: org.keycloak.authentication.AuthenticationFlowException: Unexpected state. There is no existing duplicated user identified in ClientSession
│     at org.keycloak.authentication.authenticators.broker.AbstractIdpAuthenticator.getExistingUser(AbstractIdpAuthenticator.java:120)                                                                            │
│     at org.keycloak.authentication.actiontoken.resetcred.ResetCredentialsActionTokenHandler$ResetCredsAuthenticationProcessor.authenticationComplete(ResetCredentialsActionTokenHandler.java:88)                │
│     at org.keycloak.authentication.AuthenticationProcessor.authenticate(AuthenticationProcessor.java:890)                                                                                                       │
│     at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.processFlow(LoginActionsService.java:380)                                                                                                            │
│     at org.keycloak.authentication.actiontoken.ActionTokenContext.processFlow(ActionTokenContext.java:160)                                                                                                      │
│     at org.keycloak.authentication.actiontoken.resetcred.ResetCredentialsActionTokenHandler.handleToken(ResetCredentialsActionTokenHandler.java:67)                                                             │
│     at org.keycloak.authentication.actiontoken.resetcred.ResetCredentialsActionTokenHandler.handleToken(ResetCredentialsActionTokenHandler.java:39)                                                             │
│     at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.handleActionToken(LoginActionsService.java:666)                                                                                                      │
│     at org.keycloak.services.resources.LoginActionsService.executeActionToken(LoginActionsService.java:525)                                                                                                     │
│     at jdk.internal.reflect.GeneratedMethodAccessor487.invoke(Unknown Source)                                                                                                                                   │
│     at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43)                                                                                                 │
│     at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:568)
```

### How to Reproduce?

Given there is a first broker login flow is executed, without `IdpCreateUserIfUniqueAuthenticator` or `IdpDetectExistingBrokerUserAuthenticator` steps. But only with an `IdpUsernamePasswordFormFactory` step.

When the unlinked user authenticates on the external identity provider.
And the user initiates a reset-credentials action from the login form rendered by the `IdpUsernamePasswordFormFactory`

The user receives an email but cannot reset its password.

When clicking the link a `RESET_PASSWORD_ERROR` is triggered with `invalid_user_credentials` error. 

### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Don't fail reset credentials action upon first broker login without `EXISTING_USER_INFO` (#26324)

The ResetCredentialsActionTokenHandler depends upon the `EXISTING_USER_INFO` through `AbstractIdpAuthenticator.getExistingUser` solely to log the username. However, if the first broker login flow does not include a `IdpCreateUserIfUniqueAuthenticator` or `IdpDetectExistingBrokerUserAuthenticator`, the `EXISTING_USER_INFO` is never set.

This commit does not attempt to fetch the existing user if we don't have this info set.

Closes #26323

Signed-off-by: Chris Tanaskoski <chris@devristo.com>

issue_title: HTTP status code 413 Request Entity Too Large for large SAMLResponse since Keycloak 23
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have read and understood the above terms for submitting issues, and I understand that my issue may be closed without action if I do not follow them.

### Area

saml

### Describe the bug

When using a SAML broker, when the SAML IdP sends a large SAMLResponse (via POST binding), Keycloak cannot process it and responds with HTTP status code 413 Request Entity Too Large.

I tested this problem with a IdP that uses SAMLResponses with a size of around 46 KB.

This problem did not occur in Keycloak 22.

As a workaround, I could set the `quarkus.http.limits.max-form-attribute-size=1M` in config/quarkus.properties. According to the Quarkus HTTP reference, the default value is 2048. I am not sure whether the default value has been decreased recently as with Keycloak 22 the default value was not a problem. Though a workaround exists, the use of raw quarkus configuration is discouraged and if the default value 2048, the value is too low for many SAML IdPs.

### Version

23.0.4

### Expected behavior

Keycloak/Quarkus does not return HTTP status code 413 and processes the request normally.

### Actual behavior

Keycloak/Quarkus returns HTTP status code 413, the user gets an error page with the message "An internal server error has occurred" and the request is not further processed.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Add a SAML Identity Provider that sends large SAMLResponses (around 46 KB) during authentication.
2. Use SAML Identity Provider to authenticate.
3. Observe in the network tab of the browser for 413 errors.

### Anything else?

https://quarkus.io/guides/http-reference

fix: increasing the default max-form-attribute-size (#27035)

closes: #26330
